item.title: collect_done_ids misses archived items — cleaned items cause false unmet-dependency warnings
item.intent: Ensure dependency resolution accounts for items removed via 'clean' — a sprint item whose dependency was completed and then cleaned must not be blocked.
item.description: collect_done_ids() only scans the active backlog files (backlog.json and bugs.json) for items with status=done. When a completed item is removed via 'aishore clean' or 'backlog rm', its ID disappears from both backlogs. Any item that depends on the cleaned item will now show an unmet dependency warning during pick_item and check_readiness_gates, even though the dependency WAS completed. The archive (sprints.jsonl) contains all completed sprint IDs and is the correct source of truth for historical completions. collect_done_ids() should also scan sprints.jsonl to include archived item IDs in the done set.
steps: After collecting done IDs from active backlog files, also scan ARCHIVE_DIR/sprints.jsonl for all itemId values and add them to the done_ids set
Use jq to extract itemId values from sprints.jsonl (one JSON object per line) and merge with the existing done_ids array, deduplicating
Verify the change does not break items that are both in the active backlog (status=done) and in the archive — deduplication handles this
Test: add item B that depends on item A, complete item A, run 'aishore clean', then pick_item should still treat A's dependency as satisfied for B
acceptanceCriteria: pick_item no longer shows unmet-dependency warnings for items whose dependency has been completed and then cleaned via 'aishore clean'
check_readiness_gates no longer reports missing dependency warnings for items whose dependency was archived
collect_done_ids includes IDs from sprints.jsonl in addition to IDs from active backlog files
No regression: items with truly unmet dependencies (never completed) still produce warnings
